proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.2pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.2pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.2pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is only 11/84 (13%) with no regime concentration triggered (0.5476 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-18) and no trader rejection on record.

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +33.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +33.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +33.3pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline of 54.9%) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so it is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 11/84 (13.1%) and no regime concentration is triggered (0.5476 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-18) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (94.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share falls below 85%; allow natural exits (Day 6) to rebalance, and prioritize any qualifying DOWN_TRI or BULL_PROXY signals to diversify directional exposure.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: If regime flips from Choppy to Bear or a broad-market gap-down occurs, the near-100% long-directional book on an unvalidated Candidate cohort will take correlated drawdown across all 79 UP_TRI positions with no offsetting shorts to cushion — a single-day tail event could hit ~94% of the book simultaneously.

evidence: UP_TRI = 79/84 = 94.05% of open positions (breaches 0.85 signal-type threshold)
DOWN_TRI only 3 and BULL_PROXY only 2 — negligible offsetting exposure
Top cell UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank at 10.71% (9 positions) matches only Candidate-tier cohort (n=298) — unvalidated edge
Regime split Choppy 54.76% / Bull 32.14% / Bear 13.10% — no single regime validates the long-only tilt
Sustained pattern: signal-type monoculture warning generated across most sessions in the trailing 20-day history
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share falls below 85%; allow natural exits (Day 6) to rebalance, and prioritize any qualifying DOWN_TRI or BULL_PROXY signals to diversify directional exposure.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: If regime flips from Choppy to Bear or a broad-market gap-down occurs, the near-100% long-directional book on an unvalidated Candidate cohort will take correlated drawdown across all 79 UP_TRI positions with no offsetting shorts to cushion — a single-day tail event could hit ~94% of the book simultaneously.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI dominates the signal mix at 79/84 = 94.05% (well above the 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long. The matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort for the top concentrated cell (UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank at 10.71%) sits only at Candidate tier (n=298) — unvalidated edge. Regime concentration (Choppy 54.76%) and individual cells are within tolerance, but the signal-type monoculture on an unvalidated cohort is the actionable risk.
